author: mark
text:
User directive (2026-05-20): redirect discussion item 2.

Drop the "bl init writes a real file, bl remaster flips it to a symlink" shape. Instead, make `bl remaster <url>` work on a non-initted repo and implicitly handle whatever state it finds:
- fresh git clone with no .balls/ → create pointer + materialize state-repo + set up symlinks
- bl-init'ed standalone → migrate config into the pointer/canonical split + symlink
- already federated → no-op (idempotent)

One entry point for joining/leaving a federation; bl init stays for standalone-only project creation. The discuss-first tag stands for items 1, 3–6.

User quote: "I'd rather just make bl remaster work on a non-initted repo. Why not implicitly just make it handle what it's got to?"

author: mark
text:
Symlink-based config symmetry: master.json pointer, gitignored canonical

Splits federation bootstrap out of the canonical config. `.balls/master.json`
is a small committed pointer carrying only master_url / state_remote. Under
master_url, `.balls/config.json` joins `.balls/plugins/` as a gitignored
federated-only runtime path (runtime_paths table): both are symlinks into
the hub's `.balls/state-repo/` clone, recreated by `state_repo::ensure` on
every fresh clone. The runtime stops branching on `cfg.master_url()` —
Config::load reads the canonical transparently through the symlink, so the
hub owns all policy outright.

`bl remaster <url>` is the single federation entry point: works on a fresh
git clone, a bl-init'd standalone repo, or an already-federated repo
(idempotent), and migrates the legacy in-config master_url shape. The
federated flip promotes the project's config + plugins up to the hub (hub
wins on clash), gitignores the sidecars, and commits only the master.json
pointer. `bl remaster --detach` reverses it.

Reconciles with bl-1098 (.balls/plugins symlink) and bl-ebae (federated git
hygiene / runtime_paths): bl-82a4 extends both to the canonical config and
replaces the refuse-on-drift flip with an automatic migration.
